title: serde -- Serialization Framework
description: The standard serialization/deserialization framework for Rust. Used by virtually every project that handles JSON, TOML, YAML, or any structured data format. Serde's derive macros make it nearly zero-effort to add serialization to your types.

commonPatterns.title: Rename fields for JSON conventions
Optional and default fields
commonPatterns.code:
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct Config {
    #[serde(default)]
    port: u16,                    // defaults to 0 if missing
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    api_key: Option<String>,      // omitted from JSON if None
}

commonPatterns.title: Enum serialization
Custom deserialization

whenToUse: Any time you need to convert between Rust types and data formats (JSON, TOML, YAML, MessagePack, bincode, etc.). Essentially every Rust project that does I/O uses serde.
alternatives: For JSON-only with maximum speed: simd-json, sonic-rs. For configuration files: the config crate provides layered config with serde. For binary formats: bincode, postcard.
